blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ha ha! It's fine. We have everything here. As long as we have the items in the warehouse, we can make anything. Ah, you must never tell anyone. I'm only telling you because I trust you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Gasp! Really?
Nade, who seemed like an ordinary student at school, was secretly using the warehouse items illegally.
Stunned, I followed Nade into the building.
The structure was complex, with multiple entrances. Moreover, nearly 100 buildings were interconnected, making it feel like navigating a maze.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hehe! Cool, right? But there's no need to observe it closely. We change the building's layout whenever we have time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Change the building's layout? Is that possible?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: This place's official name is the Magic Warehouse Istarus. The building itself is a magical device. It's much faster to move the building to bring items to the entrance than to search and retrieve them. It's an engineering design that considers both storage and transportation efficiency. So, on days when the school has practical training, the structure often changes. That's why no one can find us no matter what they do.
As Nade finished speaking, she giggled.
A hideout where no one could find us. Hearing this, Shirone's heart began to race.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Actually, the way to navigate is simple. But since you're not a member yet, I can't tell you.
Nade walked through the complex paths without hesitation.
After passing twenty buildings, we arrived at a blue-painted area.
One building had about ten large and small warehouses, and Nade's research club occupied the largest one.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ta-da! We're here. This is the place.
Shirone approached with a tense expression.
What kind of research could possibly require hiding in such a place?
Upon reaching the iron door, a crooked sign hung on it: - Supernatural Spiritual Science Research Club -

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Spiritual science?
Among the countless fields Shirone had considered, spiritual science had never even crossed his mind.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: How about it? Our club is amazing, right? We literally research supernatural phenomena. Ghosts, souls, the afterlife, aliens, hell, gods. Stuff like that.
Shirone's bewildered expression gradually faded. Thinking about it, the topic was oddly intriguing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The existence of gods.
Throughout history, countless people had invoked the name of God, but God had never once revealed Himself directly to the world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, this sounds interesting. It might not be magical, but it's worth researching.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! Right? I knew you'd get it! Come on in. I'll show you around.
Nade grabbed Shirone's hand and opened the door.
Contrary to the expectation of a neat space for philosophical contemplation, Shirone was greeted by a cloud of dust.
Shirone frowned at the dust bombardment.
For this much dust to rise from just opening the door forcefully was abnormal.
The warehouse was about 15 square meters.
The desk had a broken leg, and the table was littered with bread crumbs and crumpled paper, remnants of meals from who-knows-when.
They had supposedly converted the warehouse into a lab, but it seemed the only thing they'd modified was the sign.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: A bit dirty, right? You'll get used to it. Come in, come in.
Shirone couldn't understand why they didn't clean the warehouse instead of expecting him to adjust to the mess.
But such thoughts vanished the moment he saw the blackboard hanging ahead.
The blackboard, as crooked as the sign, was densely covered with equations that could make one dizzy.
A boy was still rapidly scribbling with chalk.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?
Shirone was startled when he saw the face of the boy who turned around after finishing his writing.
It was Iruki, who had been absent from classes for days due to disciplinary action.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh? Huh?
As Shirone stood speechless, Nade awkwardly smiled and explained.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! Sorry. Actually, our club has a bit of a notorious reputation, so we can't reveal our members.
That was why the eccentric Iruki was friends with Nade.
The problem, however, was the nature of the club.
It was hard to believe that someone as rational and cold as him was part of an unscientific research club.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Did Iruki have an interest in this field?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Quite the opposite. He approaches it from a critical standpoint. Iruki believes there's nothing in the world that isn't logical. So, what better prey is there than this?
Iruki seemed unaware of Shirone's presence as he continued mumbling and pacing.
Then, after briefly glancing at Nade, he approached with a surprised expression.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Huh? Shirone!
Without giving Shirone a chance to respond, Iruki strode over, grabbed his shoulders, and shook them.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: You came at the perfect time. I've just come up with something incredible! So, here's the thing: splitting the smallest matter. You might wonder how you can split something that's already the smallest, right? That's exactly my point! If you split it, it's no longer matter! Then what is it? I've been calculating for three days. And amazingly, the world! There's an incredible amount of energy contained within. If this is possible, what do you think would happen? Huh? What do you think? Tell me quickly.
His words were so fast that Shirone couldn't catch them properly.
But faced with Iruki's blazing eyes, Shirone felt he had to say something.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: W-well? Energy would be released, right?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: That's it! An enormous explosion! An explosion powerful enough to wipe out an entire city! Can you imagine it? Huh? You can imagine it, right?
Shirone was dumbfounded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But people would die. Everyone living in the city would die.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What are you talking about? It's an explosion, an explosion! The city would be blown away!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's exactly it! What would happen to the people living there if the explosion happened?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Huh? The people living there?
Iruki tilted his head as if he didn't understand the question.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You… don't mean you've never even thought about it, do you?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Of course not. What matters is how much explosive power we can generate. Do we really have to think about that too?
Shirone felt a chill.
It would have been slightly more understandable if Iruki had said he didn't care if people died.
But the fact that he hadn't even considered it was terrifying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can that be? Does it make sense for a mage not to think about the consequences of their magic?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What foolish words, Shirone. That's something for anthropologists to think about. A genius's duty is to push their capabilities to the limit.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A genius who can't help but harm others is worse than a fool.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's the few geniuses who feed the many fools. History proves it. And so does the reality we live in. Shirone, I hate it when people spout idealism.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, your logic is soaked in defeatism. Ideals can absolutely be realized!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: They're ideals because they're ideals.
As Shirone and Iruki growled at each other like sworn enemies, Nade awkwardly smiled and intervened.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, hey! Let's stop. Discussion is good, but conflict isn't. Let's sit down calmly and talk about this issue.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. I have nothing more to say. Nade, I'm leaving!
Shirone turned sharply and stormed out of the research club.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Shirone! Wait for me! How will you find your way alone?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Leave me alone. The structure hasn't changed, right? I can remember the way back. If I can't even do that, then I'm truly an idiot.
By the time Nade stepped outside, Shirone was already gone.
Returning inside, Nade frowned.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: What was that about? You could've convinced him in a way he'd understand.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Who knows. Maybe it's because we're rivals. One of us has to fall.
It was rare for Iruki to use the word 'rival.'
His arrogant personality played a part, but more than that, he simply had no interest in competition.
Neither women, wealth, nor fame could move him.
The only thing he was obsessed with was equations.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Shirone is your rival? Are you serious about that?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: You saw it too, right? The teleportation test. The bridge that couldn't be crossed.
The first person to learn teleportation was Level 10.
And to escape the Dragon's Maze, how fast do you think you'd have to think?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: According to my calculations, the smallest unit of time Shirone split in that moment was at least 1/10,000th of a second.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: 1/10,000th of a second…
Nade felt dizzy. Just imagining that minuscule gap in time made her head feel like it would explode.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: That's the mystery of insight. Faster than effort, more precise than knowledge. No conscious calculation can match the speed of intuition. But hey, Nade.
Madness swirled in Iruki's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I wonder… what about me?
Shirone's insight versus Iruki's calculation. Which would be faster?
Recalling their clash, Nade shuddered and swallowed hard.
